fields.name: defaultEventBasedHold
fields.description: The default value for event-based hold on newly created objects in this bucket. Event-based hold is a way to retain objects indefinitely until an event occurs, signified by the hold's release. After being released, such objects will be subject to bucket-level retention (if any). One sample use case of this flag is for banks to hold loan documents for at least 3 years after loan is paid in full. Here, bucket-level retention is 3 years and the event is loan being paid in full. In this example, these objects will be held intact for any number of years until the event has occurred (event-based hold on the object is released) and then 3 more years after that. That means retention duration of the objects begins from the moment event-based hold transitioned from true to false. Objects under event-based hold cannot be deleted, overwritten or archived until the hold is removed.

fields.name: daysSinceCustomTime

fields.name: daysSinceNoncurrentTime
fields.description: Number of days elapsed since the noncurrent timestamp of an object. The condition is satisfied if the days elapsed is at least this number. This condition is relevant only for versioned objects. The value of the field must be a nonnegative integer. If it's zero, the object version will become eligible for Lifecycle action as soon as it becomes noncurrent.

fields.name: isLive

fields.name: matchesPattern
fields.description: A regular expression that satisfies the RE2 syntax. This condition is satisfied when the name of the object matches the RE2 pattern. Note: This feature is currently in the "Early Access" launch stage and is only available to a whitelisted set of users; that means that this feature may be changed in backward-incompatible ways and that it is not guaranteed to be released.

fields.name: matchesPrefix

fields.name: numNewerVersions
fields.description: Relevant only for versioned objects. If the value is N, this condition is satisfied when there are at least N versions (including the live version) newer than this version of the object.

An unlocked retention policy can be modified or removed from the bucket via a storage.buckets.update operation. A locked retention policy cannot be removed or shortened in duration for the lifetime of the bucket. fields.description: The ID of the resource to which this policy belongs. Will be of the form projects/_/buckets/bucket for buckets, projects/_/buckets/bucket/objects/object for objects, and projects/_/buckets/bucket/managedFolders/managedFolder. A specific generation may be specified by appending #generationNumber to the end of the object name, e.g. projects/_/buckets/my-bucket/objects/data.txt#17. The current generation can be denoted with #0. This field is ignored on input.

fields.name: version
fields.description: The IAM policy format version.

fields.name: members
fields.description:
A collection of identifiers for members who may assume the provided role. Recognized identifiers are as follows:  
- allUsers - A special identifier that represents anyone on the internet; with or without a Google account.  
- allAuthenticatedUsers - A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account.  
- user:emailid - An email address that represents a specific account. For example, user:alice@gmail.com or user:joe@example.com.  
- serviceAccount:emailid - An email address that represents a service account. For example,  serviceAccount:my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com .  
- group:emailid - An email address that represents a Google group. For example, group:admins@example.com.  
- domain:domain - A Google Apps domain name that represents all the users of that domain. For example, domain:google.com or domain:example.com.  
- projectOwner:projectid - Owners of the given project. For example, projectOwner:my-example-project  
- projectEditor:projectid - Editors of the given project. For example, projectEditor:my-example-project  
- projectViewer:projectid - Viewers of the given project. For example, projectViewer:my-example-project

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role to which members belong. Two types of roles are supported: new IAM roles, which grant permissions that do not map directly to those provided by ACLs, and legacy IAM roles, which do map directly to ACL permissions. All roles are of the format roles/storage.specificRole.
The new IAM roles are:  
- roles/storage.admin - Full control of Google Cloud Storage resources.  
- roles/storage.objectViewer - Read-Only access to Google Cloud Storage objects.  
- roles/storage.objectCreator - Access to create objects in Google Cloud Storage.  
- roles/storage.objectAdmin - Full control of Google Cloud Storage objects.   The legacy IAM roles are:  
- roles/storage.legacyObjectReader - Read-only access to objects without listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on an object with the READER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyObjectOwner - Read/write access to existing objects without listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on an object with the OWNER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketReader - Read access to buckets with object listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the READER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketWriter - Read access to buckets with object listing/creation/deletion. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the WRITER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketOwner - Read and write access to existing buckets with object listing/creation/deletion. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the OWNER role.

fields.name: eventBasedHold
fields.description: Whether an object is under event-based hold. Event-based hold is a way to retain objects until an event occurs, which is signified by the hold's release (i.e. this value is set to false). After being released (set to false), such objects will be subject to bucket-level retention (if any). One sample use case of this flag is for banks to hold loan documents for at least 3 years after loan is paid in full. Here, bucket-level retention is 3 years and the event is the loan being paid in full. In this example, these objects will be held intact for any number of years until the event has occurred (event-based hold on the object is released) and then 3 more years after that. That means retention duration of the objects begins from the moment event-based hold transitioned from true to false.

inputFields.name: defaultEventBasedHold
inputFields.description: The default value for event-based hold on newly created objects in this bucket. Event-based hold is a way to retain objects indefinitely until an event occurs, signified by the hold's release. After being released, such objects will be subject to bucket-level retention (if any). One sample use case of this flag is for banks to hold loan documents for at least 3 years after loan is paid in full. Here, bucket-level retention is 3 years and the event is loan being paid in full. In this example, these objects will be held intact for any number of years until the event has occurred (event-based hold on the object is released) and then 3 more years after that. That means retention duration of the objects begins from the moment event-based hold transitioned from true to false. Objects under event-based hold cannot be deleted, overwritten or archived until the hold is removed.

inputFields.name: age
inputFields.description: Age of an object (in days). This condition is satisfied when an object reaches the specified age.

inputFields.name: createdBefore
inputFields.description: A date in RFC 3339 format with only the date part (for instance, "2013-01-15"). This condition is satisfied when an object is created before midnight of the specified date in UTC.

inputFields.name: customTimeBefore
inputFields.description: A date in RFC 3339 format with only the date part (for instance, "2013-01-15"). This condition is satisfied when the custom time on an object is before this date in UTC.

inputFields.name: daysSinceCustomTime
inputFields.description: Number of days elapsed since the user-specified timestamp set on an object. The condition is satisfied if the days elapsed is at least this number. If no custom timestamp is specified on an object, the condition does not apply.

inputFields.name: daysSinceNoncurrentTime
inputFields.description: Number of days elapsed since the noncurrent timestamp of an object. The condition is satisfied if the days elapsed is at least this number. This condition is relevant only for versioned objects. The value of the field must be a nonnegative integer. If it's zero, the object version will become eligible for Lifecycle action as soon as it becomes noncurrent.

inputFields.name: isLive
inputFields.description: Relevant only for versioned objects. If the value is true, this condition matches live objects; if the value is false, it matches archived objects.

inputFields.name: matchesPattern
inputFields.description: A regular expression that satisfies the RE2 syntax. This condition is satisfied when the name of the object matches the RE2 pattern. Note: This feature is currently in the "Early Access" launch stage and is only available to a whitelisted set of users; that means that this feature may be changed in backward-incompatible ways and that it is not guaranteed to be released.

inputFields.name: matchesPrefix
inputFields.description: List of object name prefixes. This condition will be satisfied when at least one of the prefixes exactly matches the beginning of the object name.

inputFields.name: matchesSuffix
inputFields.description: List of object name suffixes. This condition will be satisfied when at least one of the suffixes exactly matches the end of the object name.

inputFields.name: matchesStorageClass
inputFields.description: Objects having any of the storage classes specified by this condition will be matched. Values include MULTI_REGIONAL, REGIONAL, NEARLINE, COLDLINE, ARCHIVE, STANDARD, and DURABLE_REDUCED_AVAILABILITY.

inputFields.name: noncurrentTimeBefore
inputFields.description: A date in RFC 3339 format with only the date part (for instance, "2013-01-15"). This condition is satisfied when the noncurrent time on an object is before this date in UTC. This condition is relevant only for versioned objects.

inputFields.name: numNewerVersions
inputFields.description: Relevant only for versioned objects. If the value is N, this condition is satisfied when there are at least N versions (including the live version) newer than this version of the object.

types.name: mutation_storage_buckets_patch_retentionPolicy_Input
types.description: The bucket's retention policy. The retention policy enforces a minimum retention time for all objects contained in the bucket, based on their creation time. Any attempt to overwrite or delete objects younger than the retention period will result in a PERMISSION_DENIED error. An unlocked retention policy can be modified or removed from the bucket via a storage.buckets.update operation. A locked retention policy cannot be removed or shortened in duration for the lifetime of the bucket. Attempting to remove or decrease period of a locked retention policy will result in a PERMISSION_DENIED error.

inputFields.name: effectiveTime

inputFields.name: retentionPeriod
inputFields.description: The duration in seconds that objects need to be retained. Retention duration must be greater than zero and less than 100 years. Note that enforcement of retention periods less than a day is not guaranteed. Such periods should only be used for testing purposes.

types.name: Policy_Input
types.description: A bucket/object/managedFolder IAM policy.

inputFields.name: bindings
inputFields.description: An association between a role, which comes with a set of permissions, and members who may assume that role.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: HTTP 1.1  Entity tag for the policy.

inputFields.description: The kind of item this is. For policies, this is always storage#policy. This field is ignored on input.

inputFields.description: The ID of the resource to which this policy belongs. Will be of the form projects/_/buckets/bucket for buckets, projects/_/buckets/bucket/objects/object for objects, and projects/_/buckets/bucket/managedFolders/managedFolder. A specific generation may be specified by appending #generationNumber to the end of the object name, e.g. projects/_/buckets/my-bucket/objects/data.txt#17. The current generation can be denoted with #0. This field is ignored on input.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The IAM policy format version.

inputFields.name: members
inputFields.description:
A collection of identifiers for members who may assume the provided role. Recognized identifiers are as follows:  
- allUsers - A special identifier that represents anyone on the internet; with or without a Google account.  
- allAuthenticatedUsers - A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account.  
- user:emailid - An email address that represents a specific account. For example, user:alice@gmail.com or user:joe@example.com.  
- serviceAccount:emailid - An email address that represents a service account. For example,  serviceAccount:my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com .  
- group:emailid - An email address that represents a Google group. For example, group:admins@example.com.  
- domain:domain - A Google Apps domain name that represents all the users of that domain. For example, domain:google.com or domain:example.com.  
- projectOwner:projectid - Owners of the given project. For example, projectOwner:my-example-project  
- projectEditor:projectid - Editors of the given project. For example, projectEditor:my-example-project  
- projectViewer:projectid - Viewers of the given project. For example, projectViewer:my-example-project

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description:
The role to which members belong. Two types of roles are supported: new IAM roles, which grant permissions that do not map directly to those provided by ACLs, and legacy IAM roles, which do map directly to ACL permissions. All roles are of the format roles/storage.specificRole.
The new IAM roles are:  
- roles/storage.admin - Full control of Google Cloud Storage resources.  
- roles/storage.objectViewer - Read-Only access to Google Cloud Storage objects.  
- roles/storage.objectCreator - Access to create objects in Google Cloud Storage.  
- roles/storage.objectAdmin - Full control of Google Cloud Storage objects.   The legacy IAM roles are:  
- roles/storage.legacyObjectReader - Read-only access to objects without listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on an object with the READER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyObjectOwner - Read/write access to existing objects without listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on an object with the OWNER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketReader - Read access to buckets with object listing. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the READER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketWriter - Read access to buckets with object listing/creation/deletion. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the WRITER role.  
- roles/storage.legacyBucketOwner - Read and write access to existing buckets with object listing/creation/deletion. Equivalent to an ACL entry on a bucket with the OWNER role.

inputFields.name: eventBasedHold
inputFields.description: Whether an object is under event-based hold. Event-based hold is a way to retain objects until an event occurs, which is signified by the hold's release (i.e. this value is set to false). After being released (set to false), such objects will be subject to bucket-level retention (if any). One sample use case of this flag is for banks to hold loan documents for at least 3 years after loan is paid in full. Here, bucket-level retention is 3 years and the event is the loan being paid in full. In this example, these objects will be held intact for any number of years until the event has occurred (event-based hold on the object is released) and then 3 more years after that. That means retention duration of the objects begins from the moment event-based hold transitioned from true to false.

inputFields.name: generation
inputFields.description: The content generation of this object. Used for object versioning.

inputFields.name: id

Note 1: This field is not provided for objects with an active event-based hold, since retention expiration is unknown until the hold is removed. Note 2: This value can be provided even when temporary hold is set (so that the user can reason about policy without having to first unset the temporary hold).

inputFields.description: Whether an object is under temporary hold. While this flag is set to true, the object is protected against deletion and overwrites. A common use case of this flag is regulatory investigations where objects need to be retained while the investigation is ongoing. Note that unlike event-based hold, temporary hold does not impact retention expiration time of an object.

This includes changes made by a requester, such as modifying custom metadata, as well as changes made by Cloud Storage on behalf of a requester, such as changing the storage class based on an Object Lifecycle Configuration.

inputFields.description: SHA256 hash value of the encryption key.

types.name: mutation_storage_objects_compose_contexts_Input
types.description: User-defined or system-defined object contexts. Each object context is a key-payload pair, where the key provides the identification and the payload holds the associated value and additional metadata.

types.name: mutation_storage_objects_compose_owner_Input
types.description: The owner of the object. This will always be the uploader of the object.

inputFields.name: entity
inputFields.description: The entity, in the form user-userId.

inputFields.name: entityId

types.name: mutation_storage_objects_compose_retention_Input
types.description: A collection of object level retention parameters.

inputFields.name: retainUntilTime
inputFields.description: A time in RFC 3339 format until which object retention protects this object.

inputFields.name: mode
inputFields.description: The bucket's object retention mode, can only be Unlocked or Locked.

inputFields.name: generation
inputFields.description: The generation of this object to use as the source.

inputFields.name: name

types.name: mutationInput_storage_objects_compose_input_sourceObjects_items_objectPreconditions_Input
types.description: Conditions that must be met for this operation to execute.

inputFields.name: ifGenerationMatch
inputFields.description: Only perform the composition if the generation of the source object that would be used matches this value. If this value and a generation are both specified, they must be the same value or the call will fail.

types.name: RewriteResponse
types.description: A rewrite response.

fields.name: done
fields.description: true if the copy is finished; otherwise, false if the copy is in progress. This property is always present in the response.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The kind of item this is.

fields.name: objectSize
fields.description: The total size of the object being copied in bytes. This property is always present in the response.

fields.name: rewriteToken
fields.description: A token to use in subsequent requests to continue copying data. This token is present in the response only when there is more data to copy.

fields.name: totalBytesRewritten
fields.description: The total bytes written so far, which can be used to provide a waiting user with a progress indicator. This property is always present in the response.

types.name: Channel
types.description: An notification channel used to watch for resource changes.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The address where notifications are delivered for this channel.

fields.name: expiration
fields.description: Date and time of notification channel expiration, expressed as a Unix timestamp, in milliseconds. Optional.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Identifies this as a notification channel used to watch for changes to a resource, which is "api#channel".

fields.name: params
fields.description: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior. Optional.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: A Boolean value to indicate whether payload is wanted. Optional.

fields.name: resourceId
fields.description: An opaque ID that identifies the resource being watched on this channel. Stable across different API versions.

fields.name: resourceUri
fields.description: A version-specific identifier for the watched resource.

fields.name: token
fields.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel.

types.name: BulkRestoreObjectsRequest_Input
types.description: A bulk restore objects request.

inputFields.name: allowOverwrite
inputFields.description: If false (default), the restore will not overwrite live objects with the same name at the destination. This means some deleted objects may be skipped. If true, live objects will be overwritten resulting in a noncurrent object (if versioning is enabled). If versioning is not enabled, overwriting the object will result in a soft-deleted object. In either case, if a noncurrent object already exists with the same name, a live version can be written without issue.

inputFields.name: softDeletedAfterTime
inputFields.description: Restores only the objects that were soft-deleted after this time.

inputFields.name: softDeletedBeforeTime
inputFields.description: Restores only the objects that were soft-deleted before this time.

inputFields.name: matchGlobs
inputFields.description: Restores only the objects matching any of the specified glob(s). If this parameter is not specified, all objects will be restored within the specified time range.

inputFields.name: copySourceAcl
inputFields.description: If true, copies the source object's ACL; otherwise, uses the bucket's default object ACL. The default is false.

inputFields.name: createdAfterTime
inputFields.description: Restores only the objects that were created after this time.

inputFields.name: createdBeforeTime
inputFields.description: Restores only the objects that were created before this time.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
